script:
from pyspark.sql.functions import *
from pyspark.sql.types import *
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
from datetime import datetime
from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo
from awsglue.context import GlueContext
from awsglue.job import Job
import pyspark.sql.functions as F
sc = SparkContext()
glueContext = GlueContext(sc)
spark = glueContext.spark_session

job = Job(glueContext)
job.init("silverToGoldJob", {})
# #manual reading file
# input_path = "s3://job-market-tracker-hsg/silver/year=2026/month=02/day=20/part-00000-aa1f3e27-8161-4e00-9593-83537474b6fe.c000.snappy.parquet"
# df = spark.read.parquet(input_path)
# dynamic file reading
today = datetime.now()
day = today.strftime("%d")
month = today.strftime("%m")
year = today.strftime("%Y")

# Dynamic folder path
input_path = f"s3://job-market-tracker-hsg/silver/year={year}/month={month}/day={day}/"

# Read all parquet files in that folder
df = spark.read.parquet(input_path + "*.parquet")
df.show(3)
df = df.withColumn(
    'job_title',
    lower(col('job_title'))
)
df.select('job_title').show()
df = df.withColumn(
        'technology_text',
        lower(concat_ws(" ", col("technologies")))
)
# 1️⃣ Standardize Job Roles

df = df.withColumn(
    "standardized_role",
    
    # Data Engineer logic
    when(
        col("job_title").contains("data engineer") |
        col("technology_text").rlike("spark|airflow|etl|kafka|hadoop|databricks"),
        "Data Engineer"
    )
    
    # Data Scientist logic
    .when(
        col("job_title").contains("data scien") |
        col("technology_text").rlike("machine learning|ml|tensorflow|pytorch|scikit"),
        "Data Scientist"
    )
    
    # Data Analyst logic
    .when(
        col("job_title").contains("data analyst") |
        col("technology_text").rlike("power bi|tableau|excel|sql"),
        "Data Analyst"
    )
    
    .otherwise("Other")
)
df.select('job_title', 'standardized_role').show()
# 2️⃣ Role Demand (Overall)

df_role_demand = df.groupBy("standardized_role").agg(
    count("*").alias("job_count")
)

df_role_demand.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(
    "s3://job-market-tracker-hsg/gold/role_demand/"
)
# 3️⃣ Role Trend (Over Time)

df_role_trend = df.groupBy(
    "standardized_role",
    F.year("date_posted").alias("year")
).agg(
    count("*").alias("job_count")
)

df_role_trend.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(
    "s3://job-market-tracker-hsg/gold/role_trend/"
)
# 4️⃣ Role Demand by Location

df_role_location = df.groupBy(
    "standardized_role",
    "location_city"
).agg(
    count("*").alias("job_count")
)

df_role_location.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(
    "s3://job-market-tracker-hsg/gold/role_location_demand/"
)
# 5️⃣ Top Skills by Role

df_skills = df.withColumn(
    "skill", explode(col("technologies"))
)

df_top_skills = df_skills.groupBy(
    "standardized_role",
    "skill"
).agg(
    count("*").alias("demand_count")
)

df_top_skills.write.mode("overwrite").parquet(
    "s3://job-market-tracker-hsg/gold/top_skills_by_role/"
)
job.commit()

from pyspark.sql.functions import *
from pyspark.sql.types import *
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
from datetime import datetime
from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo
from awsglue.context import GlueContext
from awsglue.job import Job
import pyspark.sql.functions as F

sc = SparkContext()
glueContext = GlueContext(sc)
spark = glueContext.spark_session

job = Job(glueContext)
job.init("silverToGoldJob", {})

# dynamic file reading
today = datetime.now()
day = today.strftime("%d")
month = today.strftime("%m")
year = today.strftime("%Y")

# Dynamic folder path
input_path = f"s3://job-market-tracker-hsg/silver/year={year}/month={month}/day={day}/"

# Read all parquet files in that folder
df = spark.read.parquet(input_path + "*.parquet")
df.show(3)
